name: scientific-slides
tagline: Builds AI-generated slide decks (PDF/PowerPoint/LaTeX Beamer) for research talks, using Nano Banana Pro image generation via OpenRouter.
verdict: Well-triggered, concrete workflow with worked examples and sane defaults (default author, default PDF workflow). But it never says what to do when the AI-generation API, LaTeX compile, or PDF conversion steps fail; doesn't declare python3/LaTeX/library dependencies; the description omits that the default workflow uploads attached research figures to a.

highlights.summary: Description (line 3) never discloses that the default, primary workflow uploads slide prompts and attached user figures to a third-party AI API (Nano Banana Pro/OpenRouter).
No step anywhere addresses API failures, LaTeX compile errors, or missing figures/tools despite a multi-step external-dependency flow (e.g. line 1015 validate step has no failure guidance).
Design principles, common pitfalls, and 6-stage workflow (lines 342-406, 675-865, 896-976) are pasted in full inline even though dedicated reference files for the same content already exist.
